title: artifact add: default to latest tag when no tag specified
body:
Fixes issue where podman artifact add did not default to :latest tag like container images do, causing inconsistent behavior.

fixes #27083

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
@mheon would u like to look into it 
?
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

> Thanks, I've checked the code. I believe the "latest" tag should be baked into the artifact by default.
> 
> You can retrigger Cirrus CI jobs. Just open the details of a failed task (for example, at its URL ). If you are authenticated to Cirrus CI with your GitHub account, you should see a "Rerun" button. For Packit jobs, you can rerun them from a comment. [See the documentation on retriggering.](https://packit.dev/docs/retriggering). Please remove the commits that were made solely to retrigger the CI pipeline.

did Defaulted untagged artifacts to :latest at add-time (parity with images), podman artifact ls shows latest in TAG when missing , Dropped CI-only retrigger commits and force-pushed a clean history.

@shiavm006 It looks like your commit message has incorrect formatting. You can use the --amend flag to fix the text. I think fixing the escaped newlines should help. Out of curiosity, how did you create this commit?

> Out of curiosity, how did you create this commit?

I made the changes locally, ran golangci-lint to catch the style issue, then staged the fix and used git commit --amend -s to squash it into the existing commit with DCO, followed by git push --force-with-lease to update the same branch on my fork.

> @shiavm006 It looks like your commit message has incorrect formatting. You can use the --amend flag to fix the text. I think fixing the escaped newlines should help. Out of curiosity, how did you create this commit?

I’ve amended the commit message to proper subject/body (no escaped newlines) and kept the DCO. Also applied the += ":latest" nit and squashed into the same commit. Waiting on CI now.
@shiavm006 Could you please retrigger the 'Build for MacOS' job? It looks like it failed due to a timeout.

> @shiavm006 Could you please retrigger the 'Build for MacOS' job? It looks like it failed due to a timeout.

already did that
@shiavm006 Please update the tests to expect the `:latest` tag.

> Please update the tests to expect the `:latest` tag.

 updated the tests @Honny1
Why was this closed, this is still needed?
